files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 5a595ee..f09420a 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -212,2 +212,9 @@ Day 203 / Week 29 observations:
 - Social response to citation SLA was mixed: some trust gains, but repeated requests for documented enforcement, retroactive priority, and credits.
 - Week 29 adjustment: pause new R&D, reduce paid S2 acquisition temporarily, increase S2 support coverage, keep dev high, and post that existing critical tickets get retroactive priority and credits.
+
+Day 210 / Week 30 observations:
+- Cash barely recovered to $732k because operations spend reached $1.7M/week. No new R&D should start until margin recovers.
+- Cancellations worsened to 4,138, overwhelmingly S2 A (3,609). B/C churn remains much smaller relative to base.
+- Active open issues resolve quickly when joined to active subscriptions, but the dashboard backlog still grows. Current support spend is not proportionally improving churn, so stop escalating blindly.
+- S2 content at $3k/day still generated 2,395 paid subscribers, but network effects generated over 5,000 S2 subscribers without ad cost.
+- Week 30 adjustment: keep paid acquisition throttled, upgrade A to tier 2 to address low-tier value/churn, reduce ops from emergency levels while preserving heavy S2 support, and publish SLA/MTTR reporting.
